name: fixinventory-plugin-azure

metadata.name: fixinventory-plugin-azure
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Affero General Public License v3 or later (AGPLv3+)
metadata.summary: Runs collector plugins and sends the result to fixcore.

documentation:
# fix-plugin-azure
An Azure collector plugin for Fix.

## Usage
For details on how to edit configuration, please see [the documentation](https://inventory.fix.security/docs/getting-started/configuring-fix).

## License
See [LICENSE](../../LICENSE) for details.

functions.name: fix_plugin_azure.utils.rgetattr
documentation: Recursively retrieves the value from a nested class based on the provided attr path.

functions.name: fix_plugin_azure.utils.rgetvalue
documentation: Recursively retrieves the value from a nested dictionary based on the provided key path.

classes.name: fix_plugin_azure.azure_client.MetricRequestError
documentation: Error raised when there's an issue retrieving metric data from the Azure API.

class_variables.name: fix_plugin_azure.azure_client.AzureResourceSpec.expected_error_codes
documentation: A dictionary that maps specific error codes (str) to corresponding hints (Optional[str]) to provide additional context or troubleshooting information when an error occurs.

class_variables.name: fix_plugin_azure.azure_client.RestApiSpec.scope
documentation: if not provided, will be inferred from the URL: <scheme>://<netloc>/.default

class_variables.name: fix_plugin_azure.azure_client.RestApiSpec.expected_error_codes
documentation: A dictionary that maps specific error codes (str) to corresponding hints (Optional[str]) to provide additional context or troubleshooting information when an error occurs.

functions.name: fix_plugin_azure.resource.base.parse_json
documentation:
Use this method to parse json into a class. If the json can not be parsed, the error is reported to the core.
Based on configuration, either the exception is raised or None is returned.
\:param json: the json to parse.
\:param clazz: the class to parse into.
\:param builder: the graph builder.
\:param mapping: the optional mapping to apply before parsing.
\:return: The parsed object or None.

methods.name: fix_plugin_azure.resource.base.MicrosoftResource.extract_part
documentation:
Extracts a specific part from a resource ID.

The function takes a resource ID and a specified part to extract, such as 'subscriptions'.
The resource ID is expected to follow the Azure Resource Manager path format.

Example:
For the resource ID "/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/...",
calling extract_part("subscriptions") would return the value representing the subscription ID.

Parameters:
- part (str): The part to extract from the resource ID.

Returns:
Optional[str]: The extracted part of the resource ID, or None if not found.

methods.name: fix_plugin_azure.resource.base.MicrosoftResource.delete
documentation:
Deletes a resource by ID.

Returns:
bool: True if the resource was successfully deleted; False otherwise.

methods.name: fix_plugin_azure.resource.base.MicrosoftResource.delete_tag
documentation:
Deletes a tag value.

This method removes a specific value from a tag associated with a subscription, while keeping the tag itself intact.
The tag remains on the account, but the specified value will be deleted.

methods.name: fix_plugin_azure.resource.base.MicrosoftResource.update_tag
documentation:
Creates a tag value. The name of the tag must already exist.

This method allows for the creation or update of a tag value associated with the specified tag name.
The tag name must already exist for the operation to be successful.

methods.name: fix_plugin_azure.resource.base.MicrosoftResource.pre_process
documentation:
Hook method to pre process the resource before it is added to the graph.
Default: do nothing.

methods.name: fix_plugin_azure.resource.base.MicrosoftResource.post_process
documentation:
Hook method to post process the resource after it is added to the graph.
Default: do nothing.

methods.name: fix_plugin_azure.resource.base.MicrosoftResource.after_collect
documentation:
Hook method to post process the resource after all connections are done.
Default: do nothing.

methods.name: fix_plugin_azure.resource.base.GraphBuilder.submit_work
documentation:
Use this method for work that can be done in parallel.
Example: fetching tags of a resource.

methods.name: fix_plugin_azure.resource.base.GraphBuilder.node
documentation:
Returns first node on the graph that is of given `clazz`
and/or conforms to the `filter` and matches attributes given in `**node`.

methods.name: fix_plugin_azure.resource.base.GraphBuilder.nodes
documentation:
Returns list of all nodes on the graph that are of given `clazz`
and/or conform to the `filter` and match attributes given in `**node`.

methods.name: fix_plugin_azure.resource.base.GraphBuilder.add_edge
documentation: Creates edge between `from_node` and another node using `GraphBuilder.node(filter, **to_node)`.

methods.name: fix_plugin_azure.resource.base.GraphBuilder.add_edges
documentation: Creates edges between `from_node` and all nodes found with `GraphBuilder.nodes(filter, **to_node)`.

methods.name: fix_plugin_azure.resource.metrics.AzureMetricData.query_for
documentation:
A static method to query Azure metrics for multiple queries simultaneously.

Args:
    builder (GraphBuilder): An instance of GraphBuilder used for submitting work.
    queries (List[AzureMetricQuery]): A list of AzureMetricQuery objects representing the metrics to query.
    start_time (datetime): The start time for the metrics query.
    end_time (datetime): The end time for the metrics query.
    delta (timedelta): The delta over which to aggregate the metrics.

Returns:
    Dict[AzureMetricQuery, AzureMetricData]: A dictionary mapping each query to its corresponding metric data.

variables.name: fix_plugin_azure.collector.all_resources
documentation: defines all resource kinds. used in model check
